---
title: "PKNCA Documentation"
---

## What is NCA?

**Noncompartmental analysis (NCA)** is the standard method for characterizing drug exposure from concentration-time data. Unlike compartmental modeling, NCA makes no assumptions about the underlying PK model — it derives parameters directly from the observed data using numerical integration.

NCA is used to answer questions like:

- How much drug did the body absorb? → **AUC** (area under the concentration-time curve)
- How fast was drug eliminated? → **half-life**, **λz**
- How high did concentrations get? → **Cmax**
- How quickly was drug cleared? → **CL**, **Vz**

## What is PKNCA?

[PKNCA](https://github.com/humanpred/pknca) is an R package (CRAN v0.12.1) for automated NCA calculation. It is validated against Phoenix WinNonlin and Pumas.

**Key design principles:**

- Only gives correct answers to the specific questions being asked
- Automates interval selection and parameter dependency resolution
- Supports flexible grouping (subject, treatment, analyte, study)
- Handles IV and extravascular (oral, SC, IM) routes separately
- Built-in unit support, data imputation, and exclusion tracking

## Core Workflow

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A[Concentration\ndata frame] --> B[PKNCAconc]
    C[Dose\ndata frame] --> D[PKNCAdose]
    B --> E[PKNCAdata]
    D --> E
    F[Intervals\nspecification] -.->|optional override| E
    E --> G[pk.nca]
    G --> H[PKNCAresults]
    H --> I[summary]
    H --> J[as.data.frame]
```

The four objects map naturally to the questions an analyst asks:

| Object | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `PKNCAconc` | Where is my concentration data, and how is it structured? |
| `PKNCAdose` | What was the dose, when, and by what route? |
| `PKNCAdata` | What parameters do I want, over which intervals? |
| `PKNCAresults` | Computed NCA parameters with units and metadata |

## Route-specific guidance

This documentation separates IV and extravascular workflows because the route determines which parameters are meaningful:

| Parameter | IV | Extravascular |
|---|---|---|
| C0 (extrapolated) | ✓ | — |
| Cmax | ✓ | ✓ |
| Tmax | ✓ | ✓ |
| AUClast / AUCinf | ✓ | ✓ |
| Half-life (λz) | ✓ | ✓ |
| CL (clearance) | ✓ | — (apparent: CL/F) |
| Vz (volume) | ✓ | — (apparent: Vz/F) |
| Bioavailability (F) | reference | ✓ |
| Ka (abs. rate) | — | ✓ (indirect) |

→ [Intravascular (IV) examples](user/intravascular.qmd)

→ [Extravascular (oral/SC) examples](user/extravascular.qmd)

→ [Sparse PK sampling](user/sparse.qmd)

→ [AUC types and integration methods](user/auc-methods.qmd)

→ [Half-life and terminal phase](user/halflife.qmd)

→ [Post-processing: exclude, normalize, summarize](user/postprocessing.qmd)

## Not yet covered

| Topic | PKNCA vignette |
|---|---|
| Urine / fecal excretion (`ae`, `fe`, `clr`) | — |
| Steady-state shape parameters (`deg.fluc`, `swing`, `cav`, `ptr`) | — |
| Superposition (multiple-dose projection from single-dose data) | `vignette("v20-superposition", package="PKNCA")` |
| Time to steady state (`pk.tss.monoexponential`) | `vignette("v22-time-to-steady-state", package="PKNCA")` |
| Dose-aware interpolation / extrapolation | `vignette("v21-methods-for-dose-aware-interpolation-and-extrapolation", package="PKNCA")` |
| Writing custom parameter functions | `vignette("v80-writing-parameter-functions", package="PKNCA")` |
